actor_name: Congreso de la Unión — Senado de la República
description: Senado appears in 547 MEX provisions but was absent from mexico_actor_mechanism_edges.csv. Senate patch (2026-05-21) added 3 explicit functional edges for right_to_stand_for_election covering the 2024 judicial election reform. Remaining mechanisms (citizen_initiative, legislative_petition_or_participation, popular_consultation) still have no Senate-specific edges — Senate role collapses into MEX-ACT-004 (Congress) in those mechanisms.
recommendation: Review citizen_initiative and popular_consultation provisions for Senate-specific competences distinct from Chamber of Deputies. Encode as separate rows in mexico_actor_mechanism_edges.csv for future pipeline runs.

description: costa_rica_actor_mechanism_edges.csv lacks relationship_type field. All 309 CRC v2 edges are derived from provision-level authority→actor fields and functional keyword detection — not from obligation/faculty tags. Confidence is high/medium where provision text contains functional keywords, low otherwise.
recommendation: Add relationship_type (obligation/faculty/reference) to CRC actor_mechanism_edges for consistent confidence scoring with MEX.

actor_name: Congreso de la Unión — Senado de la República
description: MEX-ACT-005 (Senado de la República) previously had no high/medium-confidence functional edges. Senate patch merged 2026-05-21: 3 high-confidence edges added (PATCH-001: issues_call→INE activation; PATCH-002: submits_to→INE registration; PATCH-003: adjudicates→Citizens registration). All three derive from 2024 judicial election reform (CPEUM Art. 96; LGIPE Art. 498, 501). Actor isolation flag cleared.
recommendation: Isolation resolved. PATCH-004 (Cámara revisora → SCJN, popular_consultation) and PATCH-005 (oath administration → Citizens, right_to_stand_for_election) remain excluded pending manual verification of chamber role disambiguation and relationship_type classification.
